title: Standard workstation
description: Configure behavior = STANDARD for ordinary readiness, dispatch, concurrency, and routing without cron, repeater, or poller scheduling.
openingSummary: STANDARD is the default WorkstationKind scheduling behavior. Set behavior to STANDARD when the step should run once inputs are ready, without cron schedules, change-triggered reloops, or long-lived poller ingress.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with behavior STANDARD. Keep type on the runtime axis for the execution family you need. Bind a worker when the type requires one. Wire inputs and route fields for accepted, continue, rejection, and failure outcomes. Do not set cron on STANDARD. Choose REPEATER, CRON, or POLLER only when those scheduling semantics are required. STANDARD is not CRON — it excludes the cron field — and it is not REPEATER or POLLER scheduling.

schemaReference.title: Schema reference
schemaReference.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with STANDARD overlay applicability. STANDARD has no selected exclusive fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this behavior. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a STANDARD workstation. The misuse example shows cron on STANDARD — cron belongs on behavior CRON and is excluded from STANDARD.

links.companionIntro: STANDARD is compatible with every published WorkstationType. Bind a matching Worker for worker-backed types; LOGICAL_MOVE stays worker-free.
links.misuseNote: cron is a CRON scheduling field. STANDARD excludes it. Use behavior CRON when the step must run on a schedule.
